page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The configuration provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and Total Memory Encryption (TME). No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core and text processing capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and bzip3 decompression tests ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is characterized by a cached read-only bandwidth of 2538.93 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth of 76.87 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations, achieving 58.99 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 57.29 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds are strong for smaller models, though text generation performance drops to the bottom 25% when processing large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i-flex.8xlarge offers a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead costs for deployments relying on network-attached storage. This instance is well-suited for memory-optimized applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, text and image processing pipelines, web serving, and small-to-medium scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on an 8 Gbps baseline network connection. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong multi-core execution, Redis throughput, and small-model LLM inference. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, data processing, web serving, and moderate machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core and strong database performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon processing for database and memory-intensive workloads.

256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with a 480 MB L3 cache
